title: Kofi and the Singing Flowers
synopsis: When little Kofi discovers that the magical singing flowers in his garden have lost their voices, he feels sad and worried. With help from his diverse group of friends, Kofi learns that working together and being brave can solve any problem, bringing joy and music back to their special garden.

pages.text: Kofi loved his magic garden. Every morning, the bright flowers would sing happy songs. The red roses hummed. The yellow sunflowers giggled. The purple violets whispered sweet melodies.

illustration.description: A cheerful young Black boy with short curly hair wearing a bright blue t-shirt and green shorts stands in a colorful garden. Vibrant flowers in red, yellow, and purple surround him, with musical notes floating in the air. The background has a soft, textured paper-like quality in warm greens and blues.
illustration.alt: Kofi standing happily in his magical singing garden

pages.text: But today was different. Kofi walked outside and heard... nothing. The flowers were quiet. No humming. No giggling. No sweet songs. Kofi felt sad and worried.

illustration.description: The same garden now looks dim and muted with drooping flowers that appear wilted and colorless. Kofi stands with a concerned expression, his eyebrows furrowed and mouth turned down. Gray clouds hover above, and the flowers look dejected with downward-facing petals.
illustration.alt: Kofi looking sad in the now-quiet garden with drooping flowers

pages.text: "What happened to your voices?" Kofi asked the flowers. A tiny sunflower whispered very softly, "The mean wind took our songs away. We're too scared to sing loud anymore."

illustration.description: Kofi kneels down close to a small, sad sunflower that appears to be whispering. The sunflower has a worried expression with small tears. Swirling wind patterns in gray and white show in the background, suggesting the troublesome wind that stole the songs.
illustration.alt: Kofi listening to a sad sunflower explaining about the mean wind

pages.text: Kofi felt brave. "I will help you!" he said. He called his friends Maya, Sam, and Zara. Maya had long black braids. Sam wore glasses and smiled big. Zara had curly red hair and loved to dance.

illustration.description: Kofi stands confidently with his three friends in the garden. Maya is a girl with long black braids wearing a pink dress. Sam is a boy with glasses and a big smile wearing a yellow shirt. Zara has curly red hair and is wearing a purple tutu, mid-dance pose. All children look determined and happy.
illustration.alt: Kofi and his three diverse friends standing together in the garden

pages.text: "Let's make a circle around the flowers," said Maya. "And sing with them!" added Sam. "We can dance too!" giggled Zara. The friends joined hands around the flower bed. They felt excited and happy.

illustration.description: The four children hold hands in a circle around the flower bed. Their faces show joy and excitement with big smiles. Musical notes and sparkles begin to appear around them as they start to sing and dance. The flowers in the center look slightly more hopeful with lifted petals.
illustration.alt: The four friends holding hands in a circle around the flowers, singing together

pages.text: The friends sang loud and strong. Slowly, the flowers began to hum along. Then they giggled! Then they sang beautiful songs! The garden was full of music again. Everyone felt so happy and proud.

illustration.description: The garden bursts with color and life as all the flowers sing joyfully with musical notes dancing through the air. The children are laughing and dancing while bright rays of sunshine beam down. The flowers are vibrant in their original red, yellow, and purple colors, with happy faces and upright petals.
illustration.alt: The magical garden alive with singing flowers and happy children celebrating

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Kofi feel when the flowers stopped singing?
questions.explanation: Kofi felt sad and worried when he discovered the flowers had lost their voices.

questions.question: What took the flowers' songs away?
questions.explanation: The tiny sunflower told Kofi that the mean wind took their songs away.

questions.question: How many friends did Kofi call to help?
questions.explanation: Kofi called three friends: Maya, Sam, and Zara.

questions.question: How did everyone feel at the end?
questions.explanation: Everyone felt happy and proud when they helped the flowers sing again.

pages.title: Complete the Story
pages.text: Kofi loved his magic _____. The flowers would sing _____ songs every morning.

pages.title: Fill in the Feelings
pages.text: When the flowers were quiet, Kofi felt _____ and worried. But when his friends came to help, he felt _____.
